qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated profile picture path traversal discloses Langflow secret_key
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is an unauthenticated path traversal in `src/backend/base/langflow/api/v1/files.py` and `src/backend/base/langflow/services/storage/local.py` on `GET /api/v1/files/profile_pictures/{folder_name}/{file_name}`, while the advisory is an authenticated IDOR in `_read_flow` within `src/backend/base/langflow/api/v1/flows.py` affecting `GET/PATCH/DELETE /api/v1/flow/{flow_id}`. The vulnerable code, control flow, and missing ownership check do not overlap.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's missing ownership filter in `_read_flow` for `GET/PATCH/DELETE /api/v1/flow/{flow_id}`.
evidence: The advisory describes an authenticated IDOR in `src/backend/base/langflow/api/v1/flows.py` where `_read_flow` fails to constrain `Flow.user_id` to the requesting user when authentication is enabled.

The only qualifying finding instead targets unauthenticated path traversal in `src/backend/base/langflow/api/v1/files.py` and `src/backend/base/langflow/services/storage/local.py` on `GET /api/v1/files/profile_pictures/{folder_name}/{file_name}`.
The finding's exploit path reads `LANGFLOW_CONFIG_DIR/secret_key` via `../secret_key`; it does not involve `flow_id`, cross-user flow ownership, or the advisory's `_read_flow` helper.
